questions.questionText: A space probe travels through deep space at 15 000 m/s with its engines off. Explain why it continues to travel at this speed.

markScheme.answer: In deep space there is no friction or air resistance. The resultant force on the probe is zero. By Newton's first law, an object with zero resultant force continues at constant velocity.

steps.description: No friction / air resistance in deep space
By Newton's first law, it maintains constant velocity

commonErrors: Saying the engines keep it moving
Saying there is no gravity in space

questions.questionText: A go-kart and driver have a combined mass of 180 kg. The engine provides a driving force of 900 N. Friction is 180 N. Calculate the acceleration of the go-kart.
Which row correctly describes mass and weight?
options: Mass is measured in N, weight is measured in kg
Mass is a force, weight is not a force
Mass does not change with location, weight does
Mass depends on gravity, weight does not

markScheme.answer: Mass does not change with location, weight does

steps.description: Identifies weight changes with gravitational field strength

questions.questionText: An aeroplane has a weight of 50 000 N. The lift force is 54 000 N. Calculate the resultant vertical force and state the direction the plane will accelerate.

A 2.0 kg trolley moving at 3.0 m/s collides with a stationary 1.0 kg trolley. After the collision, the 2.0 kg trolley moves at 1.0 m/s. Calculate the velocity of the 1.0 kg trolley after the collision.

steps.description: Momentum after of 2.0 kg trolley = 2.0 × 1.0 = 2.0 kg m/s

commonErrors: Not applying conservation of momentum correctly
Forgetting the momentum of the first trolley after collision

questions.questionText: A tractor pulls a plough with a force of 4500 N over a distance of 200 m. Calculate the work done by the tractor.

commonErrors: Errors in dealing with large numbers
